rationale.R1.1: Reviewing the section centrally involves assessing whether the contribution framing around blind hybrid-LLM trust calibration is coherent.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is load-bearing because the artifact contains apparent tensions between claimed blindness and surfaced attribution surveys or routing badges.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is central because the conclusion depends on whether the reported hover, rating, and attribution data support the trust claims.
rationale.R1.6: A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and flow even if no rewrite is requested.
rationale.RX.1: The projection should preserve uncertainty where the short delegation does not specify a particular review lens.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility includes avoiding unsupported conclusions such as clean separation or general reuse beyond the reported evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within reviewing the section rather than executing edits or broader paper work.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for potential downstream harm from overstated HCI findings about trust calibration.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability matters because any projected review responsibility should be grounded in specific features of the provided artifact.
